name: Beef & Broccoli Stir-Fry
summary: Looks good

name: Chicken Teriyaki Bowl

flags.message: Fat seems low: 170g chicken thighs (~15-17g fat) plus honey/sesame would put fat closer to 16-18g, not 10g. Calories calc (40*4+50*4+10*9 = 450) is close to 480, acceptable.

summary: Fat content appears underestimated for 170g chicken thighs.

name: Tuna Gochujang Sandwich

flags.message: Macro math: 32*4 + 22*4 + 12*9 = 324 kcal, vs stated 340 — within tolerance. However, 32g protein is high: a can of tuna (~25g protein drained) + light cheese slice (~5g) is plausible, borderline ok.

summary: Protein is on the high end but plausible; minor concern only.

name: Egg White French Toast

flags.message: Macros do not add up to a real dish: 1 slice whole wheat bread (~70 kcal, 3-4g protein, 12g carbs) + 1/2 cup egg whites (~63 kcal, 13g protein) + berries (~30 kcal, 7g carbs) ≈ 165 kcal, ~17g protein, ~20g carbs, ~1g fat. Stated 280 kcal / 22g protein / 32g carbs / 6g fat is significantly overestimated across all macros.
Quantity for 'Frutos vermelhos' (berries) is missing.

summary: Macros are substantially overestimated relative to the listed ingredients.

name: Protein Omelette
summary: Looks good

name: Greek Yogurt Bowl

flags.message: Protein looks high: 200g of 0% Greek yogurt yields ~20g protein, plus ~1-2g from granola = ~22g, not 30g. Calories (250) also slightly exceed 4P+4C+9F = 4*30+4*30+9*3 = 267, which is close but the protein figure itself is implausible for the ingredients listed.

'1 mão-cheia' (handful) of berries and 'spray de óleo'-style vague portions are fine, but the 30g carbs figure assumes ~2 tbsp granola (~12g carbs) + berries (~10g) + 1 tsp honey (~6g) ≈ 28g, which is plausible. Only protein is off.

summary: Protein value (30g) is overstated for 200g 0% Greek yogurt + granola + berries + honey; realistic value is ~20-22g.

name: Shakshuka-Style Eggs
summary: Looks good — macros (4*18+4*15+9*14 = 258 vs stated 280) are within reasonable rounding, ingredients are complete with quantities, and steps are coherent.

name: Chicken Cauli-Rice Bowl

flags.message: Spinach is listed as an ingredient and appears in the final assembly but is never cooked or wilted in any step — it's added raw at the end, which is unusual for a hot bowl. Minor clarity issue.

Macro math checks out (4*40+4*35+9*12 = 408 vs stated 450, close enough with rounding). Protein from 150-170g ground chicken (~30-34g) + small contributions = ~32-36g; 40g is slightly high but within range if upper bound (170g) is used.

summary: Mostly fine, but spinach handling in steps is ambiguous and protein figure is at the upper edge of plausibility.

name: Chicken Burritos (Low-Carb Wrap)

flags.message: Macro math: 4*38+4*24+9*14 = 374 vs stated 420 — a ~45 kcal gap, slightly larger than typical rounding but still within acceptable tolerance given variability in low-carb tortillas and light cheese.

summary: Recipe is coherent and realistic; only a minor calorie/macro discrepancy worth noting.

name: Breaded Turkey Steaks + Sweet Potato

flags.message: 'Feijão verde ao vapor' (steamed green beans) has no quantity specified — should indicate a portion (e.g. 100g or 1 chávena).
Macro math: 4*42+4*40+9*14 = 454 vs stated 520 — a ~65 kcal gap, somewhat large. Protein 42g is plausible for 150g turkey (~33g) + 1 egg (~6g) + panko (~1g) ≈ 40g, acceptable.

summary: Reasonable recipe, but green beans lack a quantity and calorie total is ~65 kcal higher than the macro breakdown supports.

name: Thai Basil Chicken Stir-Fry

flags.message: Cauliflower rice / mixed rice listed without a quantity, which makes the carb/calorie count ambiguous between the two options.

summary: Macros plausible (35*4+20*4+10*9=310, close to stated 350 accounting for sauces and oil spray); recipe is coherent.

name: Sheet Pan Lemon Herb Salmon
summary: Looks good — macros check out (38*4+12*4+18*9=362 ≈ 400), 170g salmon realistically yields ~38g protein and ~18g fat, steps are clear.

name: Turkey Meatballs in Marinara

flags.message: No binder (egg or breadcrumbs) listed; lean ground turkey meatballs typically need one to hold shape, though it's possible to omit.

summary: Macros are plausible (32*4+12*4+12*9=284 ≈ 320) and 150g lean turkey ≈ 32g protein is realistic; minor concern about missing binder.

name: Garlic Shrimp & Courgette Noodles

flags.message: Fat (16g) and calories (380) are inconsistent with the ingredients. 150g shrimp ≈ 1.5g fat and 1 tsp olive oil ≈ 4.5g fat, so total fat should be ~6g, not 16g. Calories should be closer to 230-260, not 380.
Protein of 38g is slightly high for 150g shrimp (which yields ~28-30g protein).

summary: Recipe itself is sensible but stated fat and calories are roughly double what the ingredients would actually provide.

name: Tuna Poke Bowl
summary: Looks good — macros are consistent (35*4+45*4+10*9=410 ≈ 420) and align well with 140g tuna, 1/2 cup rice, edamame, and avocado; steps are clear.

name: Grilled Chicken Greek Salad
summary: Looks good

name: Shrimp Pad Thai (light)

flags.message: Macro math: 30*4 + 55*4 + 12*9 = 448 kcal, matches stated 450 kcal closely. Protein from 115g shrimp (~22g) + 1 egg (~6g) ≈ 28g, plausible vs 30g claimed.

summary: Looks good

name: Cod à Brás (light)

flags.message: Carbs likely underestimated: 100g batata palha (commercial fried shoestring potatoes) typically provides ~50-55g carbs alone, plus residual from other items, so 30g carbs/serving seems low even if air-fried.

Calories likely underestimated: 100g batata palha alone is ~500 kcal in commercial form; even homemade air-fryer matchstick potatoes from 100g raw potato + cod (~110 kcal) + 1 egg + 2 whites + 1 tsp olive oil would exceed 420 kcal if 'batata palha' refers to the fried product. Clarify whether this is raw potato cut julienne and air-fried, or pre-fried batata palha.

summary: Macros are plausible only if 'batata palha' means raw potato julienne air-fried; if it refers to commercial fried shoestring potatoes, calories and carbs are significantly understated.

name: Turkey Chili

flags.message: Macro math: 35*4 + 30*4 + 8*9 = 332 kcal vs stated 350 kcal — within rounding tolerance. Protein from 150g lean ground turkey (~32g) + 1/2 cup black beans (~7g) ≈ 39g, consistent with claimed 35g.

summary: Looks good

name: Chicken Tikka Masala (light)

flags.message: Macro math: 38*4 + 35*4 + 14*9 = 418 kcal vs stated 450 kcal — small discrepancy (~30 kcal) within acceptable rounding/ingredient variance. Protein from 170g chicken breast (~38g) matches claim.

summary: Looks good

name: Grilled Squid w/ Grelos

flags.message: Protein appears overstated: 250g raw squid yields ~40g protein, plus ~2g from grelos, totalling ~42g (close to 45g claimed but high end). Calories check: 45*4 + 15*4 + 16*9 = 384 kcal vs 400 stated — within tolerance.
Carbs of 15g seem high for 200g cooked grelos (leafy greens, ~3-5g carbs) with no other carb source; likely overstated.

summary: Recipe is coherent but carb count is implausibly high for squid + leafy greens only.

name: Atlantic Fish Curry

flags.message: Protein of 45g is implausible: 200g raw white fish ≈ 36-40g protein, with negligible protein elsewhere. Macro sum: 45*4 + 35*4 + 18*9 = 482 kcal, roughly matches 490 stated, but protein value is too high for the ingredients listed.

summary: Macros approximately self-consistent but protein is overstated relative to 200g white fish.

name: Stuffed Peppers (Beef)

flags.message: Per-serving protein of 45g (servings=2, total 90g) is implausible: 250g lean ground beef ≈ 50g protein + 30g light cheese ≈ 8g + small amounts from rice ≈ 2g = ~60g total, or ~30g per serving, not 45g.
Recipe uses 4 large peppers but serves only 2, meaning 2 peppers per serving — unusually large portion; not an error but worth noting.
Calorie check: 45*4 + 35*4 + 18*9 = 482 kcal vs 500 stated — acceptable, but the underlying protein figure is inflated.

summary: Protein per serving is roughly 1.5x what the ingredients can provide.

name: Grilled Dourada (Sea Bream)

flags.message: Ingredients list both '150g batata cozida cortada em rodelas' and a step to 'Cozer batata em água com sal' — minor inconsistency: potato is listed as already cooked but step says to cook it.
Azeite is listed twice (1 colher sopa + 1 colher chá) but steps only reference azeite once (in the molho verde); the second quantity is not clearly used.
Protein of 45g is high: ~200g cleaned sea bream ≈ 40g protein, plus negligible from potato/parsley. Calorie sum 45*4+30*4+18*9=462 vs 500 stated — ~8% off but acceptable.

summary: Minor inconsistencies in potato prep and duplicated olive oil; protein slightly overstated.

name: Frango com Curry Verde Tailandês e Arroz Jasmim
summary: Looks good — macros are self-consistent (112*4+168*4+64*9=1696 vs 1760, ~4% off) and realistic for the ingredient quantities across 4 servings; steps are clear and complete.

name: Tigela de Ovos Escalfados com Abacate e Torrada de Centeio
summary: Looks good

name: Aveia de Proteína com Banana e Manteiga de Amendoim

flags.message: Calorie cross-check: 52g protein*4 + 90g carbs*4 + 22g fat*9 = 766 kcal vs stated 780 kcal — within tolerance, but per-serving carbs (90g) seem slightly high; ingredient totals (~80g oats + ~30g banana carbs + ~25g milk lactose + ~6g PB)/2 ≈ ~75-80g per serving, so carbs may be slightly overestimated.

summary: Macros mostly plausible; carbs per serving look a touch high but within reasonable margin.

name: Bacalhau com Grão e Espinafres

flags.message: Calories don't match macros: 116*4 + 88*4 + 48*9 = 1248 kcal, but stated 1320 — small discrepancy is OK, however protein of 116g/serving (464g total) is unrealistic: 700g cod (~120g protein total) + 480g chickpeas (~40g) ≈ 160g protein total, or ~40g per serving, not 116g.

Calories per serving (1320) and fat (48g) are far too high for the ingredients listed; total recipe fat is mainly 50ml olive oil (~45g) plus ~10g from chickpeas — ~55g fat total, ~14g per serving, not 48g.
Step 3 uses '100 ml da água da lata' (canning liquid) but the ingredient list only specifies '400g tomate pelado em lata' without mentioning reserving the liquid — minor but worth noting.

summary: Macros are wildly inflated — protein and calories per serving roughly 3× what the ingredients can provide.

name: Frango Assado com Batata-Doce

flags.message: Macros inflated: 800g chicken breast yields ~248g protein total (~62g/serving), not 124g/serving (496g total). Calories per serving (1668) also implausibly high for 200g chicken + 150g sweet potato + 15ml oil per portion (~550-600 kcal expected).
Stated fat 72g/serving (288g total) cannot come from 60ml olive oil (~55g fat) + chicken breast (~10g) — actual ~16g fat per serving.
Step 3 references '4 dentes de alho esmagados' but ingredient list says '1 cabeça de alho' (a whole head) without specifying cloves — slight inconsistency.
Paprika math is off: step 1 uses 1/2 tsp, step 3 references 'restante paprika (1,5 colher de chá)', but total is 2 tsp, so remaining should be 1.5 tsp — actually this checks out. Disregard.

summary: Macros per serving are roughly double what the ingredients support; calories and protein are unrealistic.

name: Peito de Frango Marinado com Arroz de Coentros

flags.message: Protein overstated: 800g chicken breast (~248g protein) + 300g raw rice (~21g) ≈ 270g total, or ~67g/serving, not 136g/serving (544g total — more than the entire weight of chicken).

Calories implausible: 1820 kcal/serving × 4 = 7280 kcal total, but ingredients sum to ~3200-3400 kcal (800g chicken ~1100, 300g rice ~1080, 50ml oil ~440, rest negligible). Per serving should be ~800-850 kcal.
Carbs 168g/serving (672g total) far exceed what 300g raw rice (~225g carbs) can provide.
Step 3 says 'restantes 20 ml de azeite' but marinade used 30 ml of 50 ml total, leaving 20 ml — consistent; however the marinade oil largely stays on the chicken, so this is fine.

summary: All macro values (calories, protein, carbs) are roughly 2× higher than the ingredients can plausibly yield.

name: Cottage Cheese + Everything Seasoning
summary: Looks good

name: Turkey & Cheese Roll-Ups
summary: Looks good

name: Edamame com Sal

flags.message: Fat seems high: 1 cup shelled edamame is ~8g fat which matches, but if in pods (typical serving) it would be closer to 4g. Calories from macros = 17*4 + 14*4 + 8*9 = 196 kcal, listed 190 — acceptable.
Ambiguous whether edamame is in pods or shelled, which significantly changes the effective serving.

summary: Macros are roughly internally consistent but the edamame form (pods vs. shelled) is ambiguous.

name: Protein Shake (whey + água)
summary: Looks good

name: Chicken & Chouriço Skewers

flags.message: Protein of 60g is implausible: 200g raw chicken breast ≈ 46g protein, plus 40g turkey chouriço ≈ 6-8g protein, totaling ~52-54g, not 60g. Also carbs of 30g are too high — peppers and seasonings contribute ~8-10g carbs at most; there is no significant carb source in the ingredient list.
Calorie check: 60*4 + 30*4 + 22*9 = 558 kcal vs listed 580 — close, but the underlying protein/carb values themselves are off.
15 minutes at 200°C in an air fryer for chicken cubes wrapped with chouriço may overcook the chicken; typical is 10-12 min. Minor concern.

summary: Protein and carb values don't match the listed ingredients (chicken+turkey chouriço can't reach 60g protein or 30g carbs).

name: Roasted Lombinho w/ Mustard
summary: Looks good — macros check out (55*4+35*4+16*9 = 504 kcal, ~520 stated) and ingredients/steps align well.

name: Chef's Piri-Piri Chicken

flags.message: Step 3 instructs to cook the grelos for 5 minutes, but the ingredient list specifies them as already cooked ('grelos cozidos 200g'). Contradiction between ingredient state and instruction.
Azeite (olive oil) is used in step 3 to dress the grelos but is not listed in ingredients.

summary: Macros are plausible but there is a contradiction about whether grelos are pre-cooked, and olive oil used in steps is missing from the ingredient list.

name: Chicken Satay w/ Peanut Sauce
summary: Looks good — macros sum (48*4+22*4+22*9 = 478 kcal vs 490 stated) and ingredients/steps are coherent.

name: Couve Galega Chicken Sauté

flags.message: Calorie total is overstated: 45*4 + 18*4 + 14*9 = 378 kcal, but recipe lists 420 kcal (~11% discrepancy). Also 18g carbs seems high for 200g couve galega + aromatics (expected ~8-10g).

summary: Recipe is sensible but stated calories don't match the macro breakdown and carbs look slightly inflated.

name: Black Bean & Turkey Chili (PT)

flags.message: Protein is overstated: 170g lean ground turkey (~35g protein) + 1/2 cup black beans (~7g) ≈ 42g, close to the stated 45g but on the high end; more importantly calorie math: 45*4+35*4+10*9 = 410 kcal vs 450 stated (~10% off).
No quantity given for fresh coentros (cilantro) — minor, as it's a garnish.

summary: Plausible dish but stated calories don't match the macro breakdown and cilantro lacks a quantity.

name: Piri-Piri Shrimp Stir-fry
summary: Looks good

name: Turkey Taco Sweet Potato

flags.message: Macros math: 42*4 + 40*4 + 14*9 = 454 kcal vs stated 480 (within ~6%, acceptable but on high side). Protein of 42g is plausible only if 150g lean turkey yields ~32g + small contributions from beans/yogurt (~6-8g) = ~38-40g; 42g is slightly optimistic but acceptable.
'Salsa (molho mexicano)' is listed but the word 'salsa' in Portuguese also means 'parsley', which could be confusing; quantity (2 colheres sopa) is fine. Minor clarity issue only.

summary: Minor macro mismatch (~26 kcal) and a Portuguese/Spanish 'salsa' wording ambiguity, but recipe is coherent and cookable.

name: Turkey Meatballs in Passata

flags.message: Macros math: 40*4 + 20*4 + 18*9 = 402 kcal vs stated 460 (~58 kcal / 14% gap). Also 18g fat is high for 180g lean turkey (~3-4g fat) + 1 tsp olive oil (~4.5g) ≈ 8-10g fat total; stated 18g looks overstated unless turkey isn't very lean.

summary: Calorie total and fat content don't align well with the lean ingredients listed; otherwise steps and ingredients are coherent.

name: Bibimbap (light)
summary: Looks good

name: Carne de Porco Alentejana Fit

flags.message: Protein of 55g is implausible: 150g lean pork loin ≈ 33g protein + 150g clams (with shells, yielding maybe 40-50g meat) ≈ 10-12g protein = ~43-45g total, not 55g. Calorie math 55*4 + 28*4 + 18*9 = 494 kcal matches 500, but protein figure is inflated by ~10g.

summary: Protein value (55g) is overestimated relative to 150g pork loin + 150g clams in shell; otherwise ingredients and steps are sound.

name: Iogurte Grego com Granola Crocante e Frutos Vermelhos

flags.message: Calories per serving (620) and protein (42g) are overstated. Per serving: 250g Greek yogurt 0% (~25g protein, ~100 kcal) + 40g granola (~180 kcal, ~5g protein) + 75g berries (~40 kcal) + 21g honey (~64 kcal) + 10g chia (~50 kcal, ~1.7g protein) ≈ 430–450 kcal and ~32g protein, not 620 kcal / 42g protein. Macro arithmetic check: 42*4+62*4+18*9 = 578 kcal, also inconsistent with stated 620 kcal.
Carbs (62g/serving) look slightly high given 40g granola + 21g honey (~15g sugar) + 75g berries (~6g) + 10g chia (~4g net) ≈ 50–55g per serving.

summary: Macros (calories and protein especially) are overstated versus the ingredient list.

name: Frango Grelhado com Arroz Integral e Legumes Salteados
summary: Looks good.

name: Bifana Magra no Forno com Courgette

flags.message: Fat content (52g/serving = 208g total) is implausible. 800g lean pork loin (~5g fat/100g = ~40g) + 30ml olive oil (~27g) ≈ 67g total fat, or ~17g per serving — not 52g. Stated calories (1120/serving = 4480 total) are also far too high; realistic total is ~1900–2100 kcal (~475–525/serving).
Protein 128g/serving = 512g total is impossible from 800g pork loin (max ~170g protein total, ~42g/serving).
Macro arithmetic: 128*4+24*4+52*9 = 1076 kcal, doesn't match stated 1120.

summary: Per-serving macros appear to be totals or are wildly overstated; protein and fat are not achievable from the listed ingredients.

name: Dal de Lentilhas Vermelhas com Espinafres

flags.message: Calories and carbs are roughly double reality. 300g dry red lentils ≈ 1050 kcal, 72g protein, 180g carbs total; plus coconut milk light (~140 kcal), oil (~240 kcal), tomato/onion (~100 kcal) ≈ 1530 kcal total / ~380 kcal per serving — not 848. Protein per serving should be ~20–22g, not 52g. Carbs per serving ~50g, not 116g.
Step 1 says to rinse and reserve lentils but step 4 calls them 'lentilhas escorridas' — minor wording inconsistency (rinsed, not drained from soaking).

summary: Macros are approximately 2× too high; lentil quantity cannot produce 52g protein or 116g carbs per serving.

name: Frango Tikka Masala Fit

flags.message: Calories overstated. 900g chicken breast (~1000 kcal, ~200g protein), 250g Greek yogurt 0% (~150 kcal), 400g tomato (~80 kcal), 100ml light coconut milk (~70 kcal), 30ml olive oil (~270 kcal), aromatics (~60 kcal) ≈ 1630 kcal total / ~410 kcal per serving — not 1148/serving (~4590 total).
Protein 148g/serving = 592g total is impossible from 900g chicken breast (max ~200g protein) + 250g yogurt (~25g) ≈ 225g total, or ~56g/serving.
Macro arithmetic check: 148*4+36*4+40*9 = 1096 kcal, doesn't match stated 1148.

summary: Per-serving calories and protein are far above what the ingredients can provide.

name: Chana Masala

flags.message: Calorie count appears inflated. Per serving: protein 36g×4 + carbs 116g×4 + fat 16g×9 = 144 + 464 + 144 = 752 kcal, vs stated 784 kcal — within ~5%, acceptable. However, carbs of 116g/serving (≈464g total for 4) seems high: 800g cooked chickpeas (~27g carbs/100g) provides ~216g carbs total, plus tomato/onion ~40g; total ≈260g carbs → ~65g/serving, not 116g. Carbs and calories likely overestimated by roughly 1.5–2×.

summary: Macro values (especially carbs and calories) appear significantly overestimated relative to the ingredient list.

name: Frango Saag

flags.message: Protein is implausibly high. 850g raw chicken breast ≈ 23g protein/100g → ~195g protein total, or ~49g/serving — not 140g/serving (=560g total, would require ~2.4kg chicken). Calories also inconsistent: 140×4 + 24×4 + 40×9 = 560 + 96 + 360 = 1016 kcal vs stated 1060 (close internally), but the absolute protein figure is wrong by roughly 3×.

Step 4 references 'a restante caiena' (remaining cayenne), but step 1 only uses garam masala, turmeric and salt to season the chicken — no cayenne was previously added, so 'remaining' is misleading (should just be 'the cayenne').

summary: Protein per serving (140g) is roughly 3× what 850g of chicken can supply; minor wording inconsistency about cayenne.

name: Arroz de Frango Biryani Simplificado

flags.message: Protein is implausible: 700g raw chicken breast ≈ 160g protein total → ~40g/serving, not 148g/serving (=592g total, would need ~2.5kg chicken). Calories per serving (1668) also extremely high for a 4-serving meal and inconsistent: 148×4 + 196×4 + 32×9 = 592 + 784 + 288 = 1664 kcal (internally coherent), but the absolute values are far off the ingredients.
Carbs of 196g/serving (784g total) also exceed what 400g dry basmati (~310g carbs) plus tomato/onion (~30g) can provide (~85g/serving).

summary: Per-serving protein and carbs are roughly 3–4× and 2× higher than the ingredients can support.

name: Tigela de Salmão Teriyaki com Arroz e Brócolo

flags.message: Protein per serving is too high. 400g salmon (~20g protein/100g) → ~80g total, or 40g/serving — not 72g/serving (=144g total, would need ~720g salmon). Also macro arithmetic check: 72×4 + 90×4 + 26×9 = 288 + 360 + 234 = 882 kcal vs stated 920 (≈4% off, acceptable), but absolute protein is ~1.8× too high.
Fat is on the low side: 400g salmon alone supplies ~52g fat (~26g/serving) plus 1 tbsp olive oil — closer to 30g/serving than 26g, minor.

summary: Protein per serving is roughly 1.8× what 400g of salmon can supply.

name: Shakshuka de Tomate e Pimento
summary: Looks good — macros are internally and ingredient-consistent (30×4 + 30×4 + 30×9 = 510 kcal ≈ 530 stated; 6 eggs + feta + oil match the protein/fat profile), ingredients are complete with quantities, and steps are clear and coherent.

name: Tacos de Frango com Guacamole Simples

flags.message: Calorie total is implausible. Per serving listed as 1560 kcal, but protein 116g × 4 + carbs 120g × 4 + fat 60g × 9 = 1484 kcal (close-ish). However, the per-serving macros themselves are unrealistic: 175g raw chicken breast per serving yields ~38g protein, plus ~2 tortillas (~6g) and guacamole (~2g) = ~46g protein, not 116g. Likely the macros are for the entire recipe (4 servings), not per serving. Calories per serving should be ~390 kcal.
Carbs of 120g/serving is also implausible — 2 small wheat tortillas + guacamole ≈ 30–35g carbs/serving, not 120g.

summary: Macros appear to be totals for the whole recipe mislabeled as per-serving; values are ~4× too high for the ingredients listed.

name: Tigela Mediterrânea de Frango com Tzatziki

flags.message: Macros likely reflect the whole recipe, not per serving. 200g raw chicken/serving ≈ 46g protein; with feta, yogurt, olives ≈ 60–65g protein/serving, not 152g. Calories per serving should be ~600–700 kcal, not 1740.
Even as totals, 152g × 4 = 608g protein for 800g chicken + 300g yogurt + 100g feta is high; expected ~250–280g total protein.

summary: Per-serving macros are unrealistically high — appear to be whole-recipe totals or otherwise inflated.

name: Pad Thai de Frango com Ovos

flags.message: Macros appear to be totals, not per serving. 150g raw chicken/serving ≈ 35g protein + 1 egg (6g) + peanuts (3g) ≈ 45g/serving, not 87g. Calories per serving should be ~500–550 kcal, not 1155.
Rice noodles are typically soaked in warm/hot water, not cold, for 20 min — cold soak may leave them too firm. Minor technique concern.

summary: Per-serving macros are roughly 2–2.5× too high; likely whole-recipe totals mislabeled.

name: Burrito Bowl de Carne Picada com Feijão Preto

flags.message: Per-serving macros are inflated. 150g lean beef ≈ 32g protein, + ~15g from cheese/yogurt/beans = ~55g/serving, not 116g. Carbs of 188g/serving is implausible — 60g raw rice ≈ 47g carbs, plus beans/corn ≈ 75–85g total, not 188g.
Sum check: 116×4 + 188×4 + 56×9 = 1720 kcal vs stated 1840 — internal consistency is off by ~120 kcal.

summary: Per-serving macros appear to be whole-recipe totals; numbers are roughly 2× too high for ingredients listed.

name: Salmão com Puré de Couve-Flor e Alcaparras
summary: Looks good — macros (790 kcal, 60P/22C/52F) match ingredients per serving and sum to ~796 kcal; steps are clear and coherent.
